name: Afloat Mornington
signature: A floating café and bar moored to Mornington Pier — all-day menu, fish sandwiches, and the novelty of eggs on toast that bob gently on the tide
editorNote:
Afloat is exactly what it sounds like — a floating café and bar moored to the side of Mornington Pier, with deck seating on top and tables below that genuinely rock with the swell when the wind picks up. The menu is all-day and leans into the water: chowders, fish sandwiches, bay oysters, a burger for anyone who wants dry land food in a floating room.

The novelty does most of the sales work, but the kitchen backs it up well enough that the place has earned its crowd beyond the first visit. Take a coffee to the top deck on a still morning. Order a glass of something crisp on a warm afternoon. Accept that the whole thing is vaguely ridiculous and absolutely worth an hour.

A good casual companion to a more serious Rocks booking next door. Or the exact right answer on a day when you do not want to commit to anything.
